key_points: Honors Village dorm was still under construction at move-in
Campus is large — 30 minutes to walk end to end
Dorm overnight guest policy exists but is rarely enforced
key_quotes: I'm living in the Honors Village this upcoming Fall! I hope it's done being built for our move in
30 minutes to get around campus? Dang, it takes me 5 minutes to walk from one side to another in my university
The 12 overnight visits rule is an official formality that is stated in our contract, but rarely do we ever check it

key_points: Reputation as a party school is real but doesn't define everyone
Greek life is visible and influential, especially sororities
Strong school spirit and big-time athletics draw wide engagement
key_quotes: Colleges in Arizona are where kids from Laguna Beach go to frat
I didn't even think I wanted to join a sorority, but seeing your Alpha Phi videos completely flipped my view
Any public school of significant size will be a party school. It's what you make of it

key_points: No proper all-you-can-eat dining hall — a consistent complaint
Meal plan options are limited compared to peer universities
Students recommend living off-campus to avoid meal plan costs
key_quotes: On regards of food on campus, it sucks! Is the thing that I dislike the most about the UoA
Rutgers has 4 different dining halls, all buffet style. Having at least 1 proper dining hall with all you can eat will be awesome
I would live off campus in a cheap apt instead of a dorm. Don't get meal plans

key_points: One student cited rampant antisemitism and dangerous social situations
Mental health counseling services exist and are recommended by students
Some students transferred out citing mental health hitting its worst point
key_quotes: Trouble making friends, starting to hate the city, rampant antisemitism, keep getting put in dangerous situations by people supposed to be my friends, mental health at its worst
Make sure to take advantage of all the services they offer you, including mental health counseling
Active Minds is also great for mental health awareness on campus

key_points: Merit scholarships for OOS students were slashed by $15k for Fall 2025
School is in a publicized financial crisis; president resigned over mismanagement
In-state students and pre-med applicants still see strong value proposition
key_quotes: Arizona slashed their merit scholarships — max OOS merit dropped from $35k to $20k
They are in a financial crisis mode
President mismanaged $ and is resigning. The only question was how much of a reduction

key_points: Astrophysics and optics programs are genuinely world-class, often underrated
Intro STEM courses like Chem 151 and Math 112 are brutally hard
Pre-med program is strong with a medical school directly on campus
key_quotes: If he wants to get a PhD in Astrophysics eventually, U of A is the right call. Its astrophysics is one of the best in the world
If you take Chem 151 or Math 112, they are horrible and hard as hell. Get a tutor if you aren't good
Every high-achieving student I knew who was interested in pre-med went to UA. You should DEFINITELY apply for the honors college

key_points: University is in active financial crisis with leadership instability
Alleged pattern of pushing out unwanted students through administrative harassment
Housing and orientation paperwork processes require acting fast or losing spots
key_quotes: It has become official UoAZ practice to harass students they don't want at the university in such a way they have no choice but to leave
President mismanaged $ and is resigning
Put down your deposit fast. Orientation dates will fill up as well

key_points: Tucson is a college town with charm but lacks big-city energy
Heat and desert wildlife are real concerns — scorpions in apartments
Campus is beautiful and walkable but physically large at 30 minutes end-to-end
key_quotes: Do I have to worry about scorpions and poisonous spiders in an off-campus apartment?
Tucson doesn't have the big city vibes, but it is very much a college town which I think is neat
Phoenix is an overwhelming, paved-over megalopolis. Tucson is a good-sized city with lots of charm

key_points: Strong outcomes reported for astrophysics, optics, and pre-med graduates
CS program is solid but not a top recruiting target like T20 schools
Alumni include Rhodes scholars, doctors, lawyers, and Peace Corps members
key_quotes: I know a few who were Rhodes scholars, and some who are now doctors, lawyers, Peace Corps-ers
CS is one of those degrees where you can pretty much get a job out of most schools if you work hard
A degree from UA never held her back

key_points: OOS students took a major hit with recent scholarship reductions
In-state students and honors college recipients report strong value
Student turning down UW for UA Honors specifically cited affordability as the win
key_quotes: I'll be turning down my dream school of the University of Washington in favor of the University of Arizona Honors College — it feels refreshing
It's a school that I can afford, which is a big deal for me and my family
Your UArizona number may increase as they just reduced their OOS merit and have been having financial issues

key_points: Best described as a fun, mid-sized public university with strong niche programs
Reputation as a party school overshadows genuinely excellent research departments
Students who thrive here are self-directed; passive students struggle
key_quotes: A school can be a party school and still be strong academically. College is what you make it
University of Arizona for Astrophysics — most slept-on school by major
U of A is an EXCELLENT school — and I say that as someone who works at ASU

key_points: **Administration:** Alleged harassment of unwanted students until they leave
**Finances:** Active institutional financial crisis with president resigning over mismanagement
**Safety/Culture:** Student reported antisemitism and dangerous social situations on campus
key_quotes: It has become official UoAZ practice to harass students they don't want at the university in such a way they have no choice but to leave
They are in a financial crisis mode — president mismanaged $ and is resigning
Rampant antisemitism, keep getting put in dangerous situations by people supposed to be my friends

key_points: Astrophysics program rivals Ivies — PhD applicants told to choose UA over Princeton
Honors College offers a significantly different, more rigorous experience
Clubs like Vida (Costa Rica conservation trip) and Blue Chip offer unique opportunities
key_quotes: Even if he could afford the ivies, I'd be telling you to seriously consider going to Arizona — he should go to Arizona
Blue Chip is great for leadership experience and there are amazing acapella groups on campus
There is a very strong professional development culture at UA if you seek it out
